# Contributing to AppManager

> IIS 站点与 Windows 服务管理系统 — Contributing Guide

## Development Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet) | 8.0+ | Build and runtime |
| [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en) | v18 or v20 | Client-side libraries |
| SQL Server | LocalDB or full | Database |
| Windows OS | — | IIS + Windows Service management APIs |

> **Important:** The application manages IIS sites and Windows Services and requires **administrator privileges** to run. Development and testing should be performed on a Windows machine.

### Getting Started

```bash
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone <repo-url> app-manager
cd app-manager

# 2. Restore .NET dependencies
dotnet restore

# 3. Install client-side libraries
abp install-libs

# 4. Run database migration (creates DB + seeds initial data)
dotnet run --project src/AppManager.DbMigrator

# 5. Run the web application (requires admin privileges)
dotnet run --project src/AppManager.Web
```

## Available Commands

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: commands -->
| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `dotnet restore` | Restore all NuGet dependencies |
| `dotnet build` | Build the entire solution |
| `dotnet run --project src/AppManager.DbMigrator` | Run database migration and seed data |
| `dotnet run --project src/AppManager.Web` | Start the web application (requires admin) |
| `dotnet test` | Run all tests |
| `dotnet test test/AppManager.Domain.Tests` | Run domain layer tests only |
| `dotnet test test/AppManager.Application.Tests` | Run application layer tests only |
| `dotnet test test/AppManager.Web.Tests` | Run web layer tests only |
| `dotnet test test/AppManager.EntityFrameworkCore.Tests` | Run EF Core integration tests |
| `dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName=Namespace.Class.Method"` | Run a single test |
| `abp install-libs` | Install client-side NPM libraries |
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED: commands -->

## Project Architecture

The project follows [ABP Framework](https://abp.io/) 8.3.4 layered architecture with Domain-Driven Design (DDD) patterns:

```
src/
├── AppManager.Domain/                  # Domain layer: entities, domain services, repository interfaces
├── AppManager.Domain.Shared/           # Domain shared: localization resources (zh-Hans/en)
├── AppManager.Application/             # Application layer: business logic implementation
├── AppManager.Application.Contracts/   # Application contracts: DTOs, AppService interfaces, permission constants
├── AppManager.EntityFrameworkCore/      # EF Core implementation: DbContext, repositories, migrations
├── AppManager.HttpApi/                 # HTTP API layer: auto conventional controllers
├── AppManager.HttpApi.Client/          # HTTP API client proxies
├── AppManager.DbMigrator/              # Database migration console app
└── AppManager.Web/                     # Web layer: MVC controllers, Razor Pages

test/
├── AppManager.TestBase/                # Shared test infrastructure
├── AppManager.Domain.Tests/            # Domain layer unit tests
├── AppManager.Application.Tests/       # Application layer tests
├── AppManager.EntityFrameworkCore.Tests/ # EF Core integration tests (in-memory SQLite)
├── AppManager.Web.Tests/               # Web integration tests
└── AppManager.HttpApi.Client.ConsoleTestApp/ # API client console test app
```

**Dependency flow:** `Web → Application → Domain`, `Web → EntityFrameworkCore → Domain`

For detailed architecture documentation, see:
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) — Technical architecture
- [docs/requirements.md](docs/requirements.md) — Feature requirements
- [docs/ui-prototype.md](docs/ui-prototype.md) — UI prototypes

## Testing

### Test Framework

| Component | Technology |
|-----------|-----------|
| Test runner | xUnit 2.6.1 |
| Assertions | Shouldly 4.2.1 |
| Mocking | NSubstitute 5.1.0 |
| Integration tests | ABP TestBase + in-memory SQLite |
| Web tests | ABP AspNetCore TestBase + HtmlAgilityPack |

### Test Structure

Tests follow the AAA (Arrange-Act-Assert) pattern and mirror the `src/` project structure:

```csharp
public class SampleDomainTests : AppManagerDomainTestBase
{
    [Fact]
    public void Should_Create_IisSite_With_Valid_Input()
    {
        // Arrange
        // Act
        // Assert
    }
}
```

### Test Configuration

- **TestBase module** disables background job execution and enables always-allow authorization
- **EF Core tests** use in-memory SQLite (not real SQL Server)
- **Web tests** use `HtmlAgilityPack` to scrape Razor Page HTML output

### Running Tests

```bash
# All tests
dotnet test

# Specific project
dotnet test test/AppManager.Domain.Tests

# Single test
dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName=AppManager.Samples.SampleDomainTests.Should_Create_IisSite_With_Valid_Input"
```

## Code Style

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: code-style -->
- Follow [Microsoft .NET coding conventions](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/fundamentals/coding-style/coding-conventions)
- Enable nullable reference types (enabled project-wide)
- Prefer explicit access modifiers on public and internal APIs
- Use `record` for immutable DTOs, `class` for entities with identity
- Prefer `async`/`await` over blocking calls (`.Result`, `.Wait()`)
- Pass `CancellationToken` through public async APIs
- Keep functions focused (<50 lines), files cohesive (<800 lines)
- Avoid deep nesting (>4 levels), prefer early returns
- Never hardcode secrets or credentials in source code
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED: code-style -->

## Permission Model

The application uses ABP's permission system with 20 functional permissions stored in the database:

| Permission Group | Permissions |
|-----------------|-------------|
| IIS Sites | Create, Edit, Delete, ManageBinding, ManageAppPool, ManagePermissions, Backup, Restore |
| Windows Services | Create, Edit, Delete, Start, Stop, Restart, Backup, Restore |
| System Logs | View |
| Audit Logs | View |
| Permission Management | ManagePermissions |

Permissions are checked via `[Authorize(AppManagerPermissions.IisSites.Create)]` attributes.

## Localization

The application supports Chinese (zh-Hans, default) and English (en). Localization resources are in `src/AppManager.Domain.Shared/Localization/AppManager/`.

To add new localization strings:
1. Add the key to `zh-Hans.json` and `en.json`
2. Use `L["KeyName"]` in Razor Pages or `ILocalizableString` in C# code

## PR Checklist

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: pr-checklist -->
Before submitting a pull request:

- [ ] Code builds without errors (`dotnet build`)
- [ ] All tests pass (`dotnet test`)
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets, credentials, or connection strings
- [ ] New features have corresponding tests
- [ ] Permissions are registered and seeded for new functionality
- [ ] Localization strings added for both zh-Hans and en
- [ ] Database migration created if schema changed (`src/AppManager.DbMigrator`)
- [ ] Code follows project naming and style conventions
- [ ] Commit messages follow conventional commits format
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED: pr-checklist -->
